sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a dart, javelin (different from חֲנִית a lance), Job 39:23 41:21 1Sa 17:6, 45 1Sa 17:45 Jos 8:18, 26 Jos 8:26 Jer 6:23 50:42 . The etymology is uncertain: Bochart (Hieroz. i., p. 135-40) not unaptly derives it from כִּיד destruction, war; so that it would be a weapon of war (compare חֶרֶב sword, and حَرْبُ war). It might also be from the root כִּיד or כּוּדin the sense of invading, breaking in; compare גּוּד No. 2.

(2) [Chidon], pr.n. of a place near Jerusalem. גֹּרֶן כִּידֹון (the threshing-floor of the dart) 1Ch 13:9, for which in the parallel place there is, 2Sa 6:6, גֹּרֶן נָכֹון (prepared threshing floor).
